entities.name: cyclic adenosine monophosphate response element-binding protein

Pancreatic cancer: molecular pathogenesis and emerging therapeutic strategies

evidence_entities.text: Activating somatic mutations in the KRAS oncogene play a critical role in PDAC initiation and maintenance
diet-induced obesity, which has been correlated with an increased risk for developing PDAC in humans and mice and more severe clinical outcomes
chronic stress promotes PDAC through the sympathetic nervous system via β-adrenergic receptors expressed by PDAC cells and other cells in the tumor microenvironment
Obesogenic mediators and stress neurotransmitters stimulate protein kinases, including PKA and PKD, which converge on CREB/ATF1 phosphorylation in PDAC cells
~90% of PDACs harbor mutations in KRAS
The current standard treatment is chemotherapy with either FOLFIRINOX (a combination of 5-fluorouracil, oxaliplatin, irinotecan, and leucovorin) or gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel
the administration of the antidiabetic drug metformin or increased physical activity markedly diminished obesity and PDAC development induced by DIO in this model
